2603.25720.title: R-C2: Cycle-Consistent Reinforcement Learning Improves Multimodal Reasoning
2603.25720.paper_summary: Robust perception and reasoning require consistency across sensory modalities. Yet current multimodal models often violate this principle, yielding contradictory predictions for visual and textual representations of the same concept. Rather than masking these failures with standard voting mechanisms, which can amplify systematic biases, we show that cross-modal inconsistency provides a rich and natural signal for learning. We introduce RC2, a reinforcement learning framework that resolves internal conflicts by enforcing cross-modal cycle consistency. By requiring a model to perform backward inference, switch modalities, and reliably reconstruct the answer through forward inference, we obtain a dense, label-free reward. This cyclic constraint encourages the model to align its internal representations autonomously. Optimizing for this structure mitigates modality-specific errors and improves reasoning accuracy by up to 7.6 points. Our results suggest that advanced reasoning emerges not only from scaling data, but also from enforcing a structurally consistent understanding of the world.
2603.25720.paper_summary_zh: 穩健的感知和推理需要在感官模態之間保持一致性。<lb/>然而，目前的多模態模型往往違反這一原則，對同一概念的視覺和文本表徵產生矛盾的預測。<lb/>我們並不通過標準投票機制來掩蓋這些失敗，因為這可能會放大系統性偏見，而是展示跨模態不一致性提供了一個豐富且自然的學習信號。<lb/>我們引入了 RC2，一個強化學習框架，通過強制執行跨模態循環一致性來解決內部衝突。<lb/>通過要求模型執行反向推理、切換模態並可靠地通過前向推理重建答案，我們獲得了一個密集的、無標籤的獎勵。<lb/>這一循環約束促使模型自主對齊其內部表徵。<lb/>對這一結構的優化減少了模態特定的錯誤，並將推理準確度提高了多達 7.6 分。<lb/>我們的結果表明，高級推理的出現不僅來自於數據的擴展，還來自於強制對世界進行結構一致的理解。
2603.25720.author: Zirui Zhang et.al.
2603.25720.authors: Zirui Zhang, Haoyu Dong, Kexin Pei, Chengzhi Mao
